scores.notes: The response never shows actual charm.py code — only a table describing what pieces would be used (import ops, CharmBase, on.install, on.config_changed, etc.). The charmcraft.yaml is present but rendered as an inline-annotated snippet rather than a clean file. Because charm.py is absent as runnable code, completeness and runs_correctly are both penalised. The approach described (install + config_changed with direct self.unit.status assignment) is the legacy pattern, not the modern CollectStatusEvent pattern the gold standard recommends, so idiomatic scores 1. All APIs and events mentioned do genuinely exist (no hallucinations).
